name: Cuban Black Bean Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort and soak beans overnight; drain and discard soak water.
2. In a 4-quart pot, saute onion in butter until tender but not browned.
3. Add soaked beans, 4 cups water, bouillon cube, ham (cut into chunks), bay leaves, thyme, oregano, salt and pepper.
4. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat.
5. Cover and simmer until beans are tender, about 1 to 1-1/2 hours.
6. Remove 1 cup of beans from the pot and mash with a potato masher or fork.
7. Add mashed beans back into the pot and stir to thicken.
8. Remove ham chunks and dice them.
9. Remove bay leaves and red pepper, if used, and discard.
10. Add diced ham, green pepper, and rum to the beans.
11. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes.
12. Serve beans over rice and top with sour cream if desired.
